title: Activities with cannabis samples: Overview - Canada.ca
content:
## Activities with cannabis samples: Overview
- Authorized activities
- What is a cannabis product
- What is a cannabis sample
- Note about guidance documents in general
The information on these pages is for cannabis licence holders who will conduct intra-industry distribution of cannabis samples.
This content applies to the following cannabis licences:
- micro-cultivation, nursery and standard cultivation
- micro-processing and standard processing
This guide explains the requirements under the Cannabis Act (the Act) and the Cannabis Regulations (the Regulations) that apply to cannabis samples. It is meant to help you ensure that your activities with cannabis samples are compliant.
All other types of intra-industry distribution of cannabis are outside the scope of this document (for example, distribution between you and another licence holder).
This guide explains:
- who can give cannabis samples to cannabis retailers, and what they can give
- what regulatory requirements apply, including: storage and distribution requirements role of employees and contractors record keeping and reporting requirements
- storage and distribution requirements
- role of employees and contractors
- record keeping and reporting requirements
- how inspectors may assess compliance with these requirements

## What is a cannabis product
A cannabis product is defined in the Regulations as cannabis of only 1 cannabis class or a cannabis accessory after it has been packaged and labelled for sale to a consumer at the retail level.
Any cannabis distributed to a retailer, including samples, must meet all the requirements of the Regulations that apply to cannabis products. This includes:
- good production practices
- cannabis product requirements
- packaging and labelling requirements outlined in Parts 5, 6 and 7 of the Regulations
You must provide cannabis samples as sealed, packaged, and labelled products. The label on the package must reflect its contents. You must have an excise stamp on your product before distribution.
- Good production practices guide for cannabis
- Composition requirements for cannabis products
- Packaging and labelling for cannabis products

## What is a cannabis sample
A cannabis sample is a product that you give for free to an authorized cannabis retailer (provincial or territorial) for educational and marketing purposes. It is not intended to be sold to consumers.
- For the requirements or inducements, refer to subsections 24(1) and (2) of the Act
- Promotion prohibitions under the Cannabis Act

## Note about guidance documents in general
You need to read this page along with the Cannabis Act (the Act) and the Cannabis Regulations (the Regulations). If there are differences between this page and the Act and the Regulations, the legislation is correct.
For all questions, refer to Cannabis and industrial hemp contact information for licence holders, applicants and industry to find the appropriate email address.
